name: Beef Pie (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup butter, margarine, or beef drippings
1 1/2 quarts onion cooking liquid and water
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, allowing enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food that will be served without freezing.
2. FILLING: Cook onions in boiling water until tender and then drain. Save cooking liquid. Melt fat and stir in 1 cup flour, salt, and pepper. Gradually add enough water to the reserved onion cooking liquid to make 1 1/2 quarts. Slowly stir in onion cooking liquid and water. Add gravy seasoning if desired.
3. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly. Pour gravy over onions, peas, and beef. Mix gently. Divide one-fourth of the mixture into each baking pan and spread evenly to avoid air pockets.
4. CRUST: Combine flour and salt. Mix in margarine only until the mixture is crumbly. Add cold water and mix lightly. Divide dough into four parts and roll each part out on a lightly floured surface into an 8x8-inch square. Fit over filling in pans and sprinkle with poppy seeds if desired (using about 1/4 teaspoon per pan).
5. TO SERVE WITHOUT FREEZING: Preheat the oven to 450°F. Bake for 45 minutes or until the crust is lightly browned.
6. TO FREEZE: Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food. Fold edges together several times so that the paper lies directly on top of the food. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape. Label the food, date frozen, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months).
7. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
8. TO HEAT FROZEN PIE: Preheat the oven to 450°F. Remove freezer wrap and place the food in a baking pan. Bake for 1 hour or until the filling is bubbly at the edges, the crust is lightly browned, and the food is hot in the center.
